title: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 7.2 — Competence evidence: role-based training completion
exclusions: Does NOT assess training effectiveness; attests that role-required modules are completed within policy cadence by each person in the role.
evidence: Saved search 'iso_27001_7_2_competence' (daily) emits per person: user, role, required_modules, completed_modules, compliance_pct, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Clause 7.2 Competence — directly evidenced by role-based module completion.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A user missing a required module shows compliance_pct<100 in the next daily run.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A user with all required modules completed within cadence is absent.

spl:
index=hr sourcetype=hr:role_assignment earliest=-30d
| stats latest(role) AS role BY user
| lookup iso_27001_training_matrix.csv role OUTPUT required_modules
| eval required_modules=split(required_modules,",")
| join user
  [search index=hr sourcetype=lms:completion status=completed earliest=-365d
   | stats values(module) AS completed_modules BY user]
| eval covered=mvmap(required_modules, if(isnotnull(mvfind(completed_modules,x)),1,0))
| eval compliance_pct=round(100*sum(covered)/mvcount(required_modules),0)
| where compliance_pct<100
| table user role required_modules completed_modules compliance_pct
description: Joins the role-to-module matrix against LMS completions to report each person's coverage. Clause 7.2 requires the organisation to determine and maintain competence.
value: Replaces the annual LMS export with a daily compliance signal. HR can notify managers proactively rather than after the audit finding.
implementation: (1) Onboard LMS completions; (2) maintain iso_27001_training_matrix.csv per role; (3) schedule UC daily; (4) compliance_pct<100 triggers an HR email; (5) quarterly report to ISMS committee. Indexes required: `index=hr`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=hr:role_assignment`, `sourcetype=hr:role_assignment;`, `sourcetype=lms:completion`, `sourcetype=lms:completion;`.
visualization: Stacked bar by role, table of sub-100% users, single value 'users below 100%'.
knownFalsePositives: New hires in their grace window (30d) appear as non-compliant — exclude by hire_date.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=hr` sourcetype=lms:completion; `index=hr` sourcetype=hr:role_assignment; required-module matrix `iso_27001_training_matrix.csv`..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Onboard LMS completions; (2) maintain iso_27001_training_matrix.csv per role; (3) schedule UC daily; (4) compliance_pct<100 triggers an HR email; (5) quarterly report to ISMS committee.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=hr sourcetype=hr:role_assignment earliest=-30d
| stats latest(role) AS role BY user
| lookup iso_27001_training_matrix.csv role OUTPUT required_modules
| eval required_modules=split(required_modules,",")
| join user
  [search index=hr sourcetype=lms:completion status=completed earliest=-365d
   | stats values(module) AS completed_modules BY user]
| eval covered=mvmap(required_modules, if(isnotnull(mvfind(completed_modules,x)),1,0))
| eval compliance_pct=round(100*sum(covered)/mvcount(required_modules),0)
| where compliance_pct<100
| table user role required_modules completed_modules compliance_pct
```

Understanding this SPL

**ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 7.2 — Competence evidence: role-based training completion** — Replaces the annual LMS export with a daily compliance signal. HR can notify managers proactively rather than after the audit finding.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=hr` sourcetype=lms:completion; `index=hr` sourcetype=hr:role_assignment; required-module matrix `iso_27001_training_matrix.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: hr; **sourcetype**: hr:role_assignment. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=hr, sourcetype=hr:role_assignment, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` aggregates events by **user**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **required_modules** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **covered** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **compliance_pct** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where compliance_pct<100` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: completed_modules, compliance_pct, covered, required_modules, role, user, user].

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Stacked bar by role, table of sub-100% users, single value 'users below 100%'.
grandmaExplanation: We check on joins the role-to-module matrix against lms completions to report each person's coverage. clause 7.2 requires the organisation to determine and maintain competence. This helps maintain steady compliance posture and reduces manual review effort.
